decisions.rationale: Most precise and AI-readable name. Notification is too broad. MessageCampaign is heavier and less operationally clear.
Layer 0 bugs are documented and marked CONFLICTING. Green-ai must not reproduce them. Compat-mode only if Architect explicitly requires it.

concrete_cases.layer0_bug: GatewayApiCallbackAsync uses int.TryParse on SmsLog.Id (which is long). Silent failure for IDs > 2,147,483,647.
concrete_cases.green_ai_fix: All delivery record IDs must be long / strongly-typed ID. int.TryParse is FORBIDDEN for DeliveryRecord IDs.

decisions.rationale: Multiple writers: SmsGatewayStatusWriter (sync), UpdateStatusOnSmsLogsAsync (async DLR), batch processes. No mutex/versioning on StatusCode in Layer 0.
green_ai_requirements: Multiple writers are valid and expected
Status model must be priority-merge based: Final > Billable > Latest (BR001)
Callbacks may arrive out of order — status resolution must be deterministic

decisions.rationale: Azure Batch internals are not in Layer 0. Domain rules are complete enough for rebuild. Green-ai should not be bound to Azure Batch as legacy execution model.
decisions.note_for_rebuild: Legacy used external batch execution. Internals not fully extracted. Rebuild may choose different scheduler/runtime (e.g. Hangfire, background service, or hosted IHostedService).

decisions.rationale: DeliveryStatusEvent (SmsLogStatus) is written by all 4 channels: SMS, Email, Voice, Eboks. It is shared messaging infrastructure, not local SMS detail.
green_ai_requirements: DeliveryStatusEvent must be modelled centrally, not per-channel
Channel column or discriminator required to identify source
No channel-specific forks in status write logic

decisions.rationale: BR001 is not an implementation detail but a core delivery state invariant. Green-ai must not reduce this to last-write-wins.
decisions.rule: IsFinal(3) > IsBillable(2) > default(1), ThenBy DateReceivedUtc ascending, Last() wins.

decisions.topic: Idempotency on Broadcast activation and sent-marking
decisions.rationale: Active guard and MarkSent guard are domain invariants, not just guard clauses.
green_ai_requirements: Broadcast activation must be idempotent: repeat-activate is a no-op
MarkSent must be idempotent: only set DateSentUtc if null

decisions.rationale: Layer 0 DeactivatePlannedMessage destructively deletes SmsLogs. This is legacy behavior — Green-ai must take explicit stance.
decisions.status: OPEN — Architect must decide at rebuild phase
